title: BTS Reunites: RM's Musical Journey
content:
The K-pop phenomenon BTS has officially reunited, embarking on a personal musical journey of self-discovery set against the backdrop of a postapocalyptic world. Recently, fans celebrated as all members gathered together, showcasing their unique personalities and enduring bond. For instance, V famously showed his silly side by throwing out the first pitch at a Los Angeles Dodgers game in 2025. Other members have also been active globally; Suga attended Paris Fashion Week in early 2023, while Jin recently spoke at a 2025 press conference for Netflix’s new show, Kian’s Bizarre B&B. The group’s highly anticipated reunion follows years of individual pursuits and immense global success since their debut.

At the heart of the group is their official leader, RM, formerly known as Rap Monster. He has written the lyrics and had a significant hand in the composition and production of many of BTS’s biggest hits. RM was the first of the boys scouted by BTS founder Bang Si-hyuk in 2010. At that time, he was just a high school freshman making a name for himself as a battle rapper in Seoul’s underground music scene. When faced with criticism from the hip-hop community for joining a mainstream idol band, the versatile performer quickly snapped back.

On his 2015 solo mixtape, aptly titled RM, he declared, “I’m not pop, I’m not rock, I’m not funk, I’m not R&B or hip-hop.” Known for his fast-paced, distinctive flow, RM has successfully collaborated with prominent international artists such as Wale, Warren G, and Fall Out Boy. Furthermore, as the only member of BTS who speaks fluent English, he famously taught himself the language by watching all ten seasons of the U.S. sitcom Friends. He also briefly studied in New Zealand, which helped broaden his worldview and shape his perspective.

Beyond his musical talents, RM is known as a compassionate advocate for youth and a peacemaker within the band. He powerfully articulated his inclusive vision when addressing the UN General Assembly during a landmark speech in New York City. There, he encouraged young people globally, stating, “No matter who you are, where you’re from, your skin color, gender identity: just speak yourself.” As BTS embarks on their new era, RM’s leadership and steadfast commitment to authenticity remain central to the group's massive global appeal.
